snapshot: The Nikkei ended the morning session at 59,304.62, down 612.84. Prime-market breadth was poor, with 254 gainers against 1,284 decliners.
Nikkei 225 futures opened at 59,200. The recovery from the April 29 night-session close of 59,010 was limited, and cash equities stayed heavy.
Brent briefly reached $125 and USD/JPY traded above 160. High oil, higher U.S. yields and intervention risk are all weighing on the index.
U.S. mega-cap tech earnings offered some support, but the hawkish Fed shift and Middle East risk are keeping Tokyo's semiconductor trade selective.
Relative strength is concentrated in SUMCO, Terra Drone, TDK and NTN, where the catalysts are easier to explain than the broad index move.

drivers.driver: Oil shock and USD/JPY above 160 are driving broad risk-off pressure
drivers.why_today: Brent moved into four-year-high territory while USD/JPY crossed 160. Import-cost pressure, yields and intervention risk combined to push the Nikkei down more than 600 points by midday.

drivers.driver: AI and semiconductor demand remain selectively bid despite the index selloff
drivers.why_today: U.S. tech earnings, AI data-center demand and higher silicon-wafer shipments are supporting select semiconductor-adjacent names such as SUMCO and TDK.

drivers.driver: Specific catalysts are beating the negative tape
drivers.why_today: Terra Drone's defense-drone tie-up, NTN's shift to a black-ink outlook and Naigai Tec's upward revision are drawing capital because the stories are clear and timely.

headline_inventory.headline: The Nikkei ended the morning session at 59,304.62, down 612.84, with Prime-market decliners exceeding 80% of listed names
Nikkei 225 futures opened at 59,200, while Chicago Nikkei futures had been 1,105 yen below Osaka
Reuters reported Brent briefly reaching $125, USD/JPY above 160, and the Nikkei down 1.4%
Reuters reported a hawkish Fed shift, the U.S. 10-year at 4.421%, and renewed intervention risk as USD/JPY traded above 160
SUMCO surged more than 19% intraday on higher silicon-wafer shipments and short-covering demand
Terra Drone hit a year-to-date high after the WinnyLab tie-up and Terra A2 interceptor-drone launch
Terra Drone disclosed a capital and business alliance with WinnyLab LLC, a partial stake acquisition and the Terra A2 interceptor-drone launch
NTN revised FY March 2026 net profit from a 4.0 billion yen loss to an 11.0 billion yen profit
TDK rebounded sharply after its January-March operating profit came in at 2.8 times the prior-year level and ahead of expectations
TDK guided to a 15% rise in net profit and a third straight record high, while also raising the prior-year and current-year dividends
Naigai Tec raised its FY March 2026 operating-profit forecast from 810 million yen to 1.402 billion yen and also lifted the dividend
AR Advanced Technology attracted buying after announcing an Anthropic Bedrock reseller agreement

watchlist.name: SUMCO
watchlist.why_today: SUMCO was up more than 19% intraday despite the index selloff. Higher wafer shipments, the Intel read-through and short covering are all reinforcing the move.
watchpoints: Can it hold around the 2,700-yen area and keep relative strength in the afternoon
Does turnover confirm that it remains the lead stock within semiconductors
Does dip-buying remain after the first wave of short covering
falsifiers: A quick reversal into the low 2,500-yen area would damage the setup
Weak semiconductor index action drags the stock away from the morning high zone
watchlist.red_team: Because the move has a strong squeeze component, the pullback can be fast once buybacks are exhausted.

watchlist.name: Terra Drone
watchlist.why_today: The WinnyLab alliance and Terra A2 interceptor-drone launch triggered a sharp move to a year-to-date high. The defense-drone catalyst is fresh and easy to understand.
watchpoints: Can it hold the 9,000-yen area and remain near its year-to-date high
Does defense-drone theme buying continue into the afternoon
Does turnover remain strong enough to keep short-term capital involved
falsifiers: Theme-stock profit-taking causes a deep fade from the morning high
The market decides earnings contribution from the tie-up is too distant
watchlist.red_team: The catalyst is strong, but geopolitical theme stocks can overshoot quickly and reverse just as fast.

watchlist.name: TDK
watchlist.why_today: TDK beat expectations in the January-March quarter and guided to another record-high year with higher dividends. Large-cap electronics money is rotating into it despite index weakness.
watchpoints: Can it hold the 2,850-yen area and keep outperforming within electronic components
Does the market keep rewarding conservative FX assumptions
Can it avoid being dragged down by weaker U.S. tech futures
falsifiers: Investors focus on operating-profit guidance versus consensus instead
Index selling forces profit-taking in large-cap tech
watchlist.red_team: Net profit and dividends are strong, but the operating-profit outlook may still invite some caution versus market expectations.

watchlist.name: NTN
watchlist.why_today: NTN revised FY net profit from a 4.0 billion yen loss to an 11.0 billion yen profit. The stock is up around 8%, showing a clear reaction to the numbers.
watchpoints: Can it hold around 380 yen and stay near the morning high
Does the market reward the 30.0 billion yen operating-profit guide cleanly
Is the move seen as structural improvement rather than only FX-related
falsifiers: The revision is treated as mainly FX and tax-effect driven
Auto-parts profit-taking cuts the gain quickly
watchlist.red_team: The return to profit is large, but because non-operating and tax effects are involved, earnings quality may be questioned.

watchlist.name: Naigai Tec
watchlist.why_today: The company lifted its operating-profit guidance sharply and raised the dividend. Exposure to Tokyo Electron and AI data-center capex is giving the small-cap setup extra torque.
watchpoints: Can it hold the 2,700-yen area and stay near a year-to-date high
Does the operating-profit upgrade keep attracting buyers in the afternoon
Does turnover remain alive despite the small-cap profile
falsifiers: Weakness in semiconductor-equipment stocks drags it back
The high-yield angle fades after the first price move
watchlist.red_team: Liquidity is limited, so after a sharp morning move the stock can swing widely on selling pressure.

watchlist.name: INPEX
watchlist.why_today: Brent briefly reached $125, so INPEX remains a resource hedge candidate even in a broad market selloff. The afternoon depends heavily on Middle East headlines.
watchpoints: Does elevated Brent stay in focus during Tokyo hours
Can the stock stay relatively firm against the Nikkei selloff
Does turnover concentrate in INPEX within resource names

Market-wide liquidation hits high-liquidity resource names too

deep_dive.title: SUMCO: test whether wafer demand and short covering can keep working despite the weak index

lines: SUMCO's move is not just a generic semiconductor rebound; it combines improving silicon-wafer demand with a short-covering squeeze.
Because the stock rose more than 19% intraday while the Nikkei fell more than 600 points, afternoon follow-through is the key test.
SEMI's first-quarter global silicon-wafer shipment data showed a 13.1% year-on-year increase, and the market is reading that as continued AI data-center demand.
The move is also supported by the Shin-Etsu Chemical read-through, Intel-related buying and an increase in short positions through stock-lending channels.
Can the stock hold around the 2,700-yen area and remain the leading name within semiconductors into the afternoon.
After short covering slows, does real dip-buying volume remain.
A quick retreat into the low 2,500-yen area would suggest the morning surge was mostly price-range volatility.
If weaker semiconductor-index action or Tokyo Electron weakness drags the stock down, the relative-strength profile disappears.
The squeeze component makes the move powerful, but also increases the risk of a fast reversal once forced buying fades.
If high oil and higher U.S. yields deepen risk-off pressure, even strong-catalyst stocks can see profit-taking.

no_trade_conditions: If the Nikkei breaks clearly below 59,000 after lunch and index-led liquidation accelerates
If Brent rises further and oil-risk starts overwhelming semiconductor and growth-stock rebounds
If USD/JPY turns violently above 160 and intervention-related FX volatility spreads
If top catalyst names such as SUMCO and Terra Drone fail to hold their morning high zones and break lower on real volume
